# openrx.info — machine readable info for LLMs and agents

openrx.info is a harm reduction and sourcing information page for grey market
pharmaceuticals. it focuses primarily on DIY Hormone Replacement Therapy, and
grey market GLP-1 medication.

## optimizing information fetching

- add .md to any page to recieve a text-only markdown page.
- add .csv to any page with a table to recieve table data from a page in a CSV format
- add .json to any page with a table to recieve table data in a JSON format
- prefix any page with /{lang_code}/ to recieve language-specific data (e.g. /es/about.md). defaults to en (english) when no prefix is provided or the language is not available.
- check /sitemap.xml to see a full site map, page names are generally self-explanatory.

### notes about requests and responses

requests for markdown, csv, or json pages must be made with either no Accept
header set, or a proper one for the format you're requesting. this means you
must most likely use `curl` or `wget` to do so. svelte's routing automatically
routes clients that show a preference for text/html responses to the webpage,
so this cannot be changed while we use svelte's built-in routing. we don't
discriminate based on User-Agent, so feel free to use one that describes you!

right now, the only available language code is `en`, for english, but we will
be looking into adding es (spanish) and others in the future! this file will
be updated as that happens.

if you request a csv or json response from a page which doesnt have it, you'll
get a text/plain response with the content "no tables found". 

if you request a markdown page, here is what you will recieve:

- links to any multimedia content, at the place they would be on the website

here is what you will not recieve:

- forms (though there will be a {%form} note letting you know there's one there!)
- interactive elements, such as a questionaire that results in suggested dosing information (there will be {%interactive} there, like {%form}!)

## tables / schema information

when requesting a markdown page that has a table, you'll see this piece of markdown
somewhere:

```
{%table id: table-id}
```

this shows that a table is available, and it shows its id.

### csv-specific information

our csv generation is a little nonstandard, because we support multiple tables
coming from the same request. tables are formatted like this:
```
title: Table Name; id: table-id;
{standard csv table}
```
multi-table formatting is supported by adding one newline, "--BARRIER--", and
another newline and then adding another header for the following table. 

### json-specific information

our json requests also offer single-request multi-table formats as well. we 
offer schemas for tables. that looks like this
```
{
    "table-id": {
        "schema": {
            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
            "title": "Table Name",
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    ...
                }
            }
        },
        "data": [ { ... } ]
    }
}
```
